title: Version bump 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 + Phase 5 badge freeze unit test
predecessor: M35-T7 shipped 2026-04-20 (commit cb0ab4c — 'docs(m35): M35 T7 — CHANGELOG [Unreleased] → [1.1.0]'). Promoted [Unreleased] (M30 + M31 + M32 + M33 + M33 F3/F4 + M34 entries) into [1.1.0] — 2026-04-20 under new 'Phase 5 — Intelligence Layer' narrative header; reset [Unreleased] to six-category scaffold; introduced bottom-of-file version-link refs for [Unreleased] / [1.1.0] / [1.0.0]. Zero code / zero tests. Unit baseline 1162 preserved. Predecessors T0 (da4ce1f plan doc) → T1 (b68d09b perf defaults held) → T2 (1108247 cross-milestone E2E) → T3 (51393f8 audit view chips) → T4 (0f8b51c retrospective) → T5 (3cffe29 demo walkthrough) → T6 (a966deb README sweep) → T7 (cb0ab4c CHANGELOG promotion).
goal: Bump the repository version from 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 across every package.json that carries a version (workspace root + apps/desktop + 5 packages/* — confirm via find before edit; plan doc §3 T8 row lists 7 files and uses the literal phrase 'all 6' which was a plan-authoring drift). Add a unit test pinning the top-bar badge literal string 'Phase 5' so an accidental bump to 'Phase 6' before Phase 6 work officially begins is caught by CI. Verify no other file (docs, comments, strings, constants) references the OLD 1.0.0 version in a way that would semantically claim the v1.0.0 release marker. Tests delta: +2 unit (1162 → 1164).
acceptance: Every package.json in the workspace with a `version` field flips 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 atomically. Concretely: `package.json` (root), `apps/desktop/package.json`, `packages/shared-types/package.json`, `packages/role-schema/package.json`, `packages/provider-router/package.json`, `packages/telemetry-core/package.json`, `packages/intelligence/package.json`. If `find` surfaces additional package.json files with a `version` field, they get bumped too — the goal is repo-wide consistency, not a fixed file list.

One new unit test pinning the literal top-bar badge string to 'Phase 5' (not 'Phase 6', not 'Phase 5.x'). Placement: extend `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/components/app/top-bar.test.tsx` if it exists, otherwise NEW file with that name. Test asserts the badge renders with textContent === 'Phase 5'. Accuracy: the badge component lives in the top-bar — verify exact import path during authoring; do not invent.

One new unit test pinning the desktop package.json `version` field to '1.1.0'. Placement: a small repo-level vitest spec that reads `apps/desktop/package.json` (or root `package.json`) and asserts `version === '1.1.0'`. Together with #2 this is the +2 testsDelta.

Grep audit: scan repo for literal '1.0.0' occurrences. Expected survivals: CHANGELOG.md [1.0.0] release marker + version-link ref (both correct and must be preserved), retrospective metrics mentions, docs referencing v1.0.0 as a historical baseline. Flag any occurrence that claims the CURRENT release is 1.0.0 — fix in-place. Examples to watch: landing-site `docs/site/index.html` if it prints a version badge, README.md version badges if any, any in-code constant.
Atomic commit: `chore(m35): M35 T8 — v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 + Phase 5 badge pin`.

Ledger commit: `chore(loki): M35 T8 — commit ledger (<sha>)` — roll orchestrator.json tasksCompleted 8 → 9 + inflight {taskId: M35-T9, lastShipped: M35-T8, lastShippedCommit: <sha>}, update pending.json T8 status pending → shipped with commit + shippedAt + verificationGates, overwrite current-task.json to M35-T9 (regression hardening — flaky-test audit + stable selector sweep).

Tests green after bump: full vitest run (1162 + 2 = 1164) and Electron rebuild + Playwright (11 spec files / 12 cases) stay green. The Playwright pass matters specifically because the Phase 5 badge assertion is live in 5 specs (smoke / ticket-flow / meeting-flow / vault-backup / copilot-ui); a stale badge regression would surface here before landing.
Typecheck clean across all 6 workspace packages after the bump. Lint 0 errors / ≤24 warnings preserved.

ABI rebuild dance: (a) Node ABI before vitest: `cd node_modules/.pnpm/better-sqlite3@11.10.0/node_modules/better-sqlite3 && npm run install`; (b) Electron ABI before Playwright: `pnpm -F @team-x/desktop exec electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3,keytar`. This is the verified M31+M32+M33 recipe; skip it and the first test suite burns 23+ NODE_MODULE_VERSION failures.
filesTouched: apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/lib/__tests__/version.test.ts (NEW — package.json version pin, path subject to confirmation during authoring)
carryForward: M35 T7 closed: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] → [1.1.0] promotion under 'Phase 5 — Intelligence Layer' header; six-category [Unreleased] scaffold; bottom-of-file version-link refs introduced. Commit cb0ab4c. Zero tests. 8 of 11 M35 tasks shipped; 3 remaining (T8 / T9 / T10).

M28 + M29 CHANGELOG entries ARE MISSING from [1.1.0] — surfaced during T7 acceptance pass but deliberately NOT backfilled per 'DO NOT invent' carry-forward rule. If Phase 6 T0 or a retrospective patch wants to backfill them into [1.1.0] retroactively, that's a future-phase decision. The release header-level scope statement ('RAG foundation (M28), RAG into agent turns (M29)') preserves scope faithfulness without claiming content that was never authored.
Test baseline current at 1162 unit / 11 E2E specs / 12 Playwright cases (as of M35 T3). T8 target +2 unit → 1164. T9 target +2 unit → 1166. T10 verification is the final gate.

Phase 5 badge FROZEN at 'Phase 5' through v1.1.0 release. DO NOT bump to 'Phase 6' in the top-bar component — that is a Phase 6 T0 concern. The badge-pin unit test this task adds is the insurance against an accidental premature bump.

v1.0.0 shipped 2026-04-13 as the Phase 4 ship-readiness release (CLAUDE.md M27 marker); v1.1.0 is the Phase 5 Intelligence Layer release — additive (RAG + NLU + agentic + planner + copilot + copilot UI + demo hardening), zero breaking changes, minor version bump per semver. Reinforces the T7 CHANGELOG framing.

ABI state at start of T8: Node (post M35 T7 — T7 was docs-only, no rebuild). T8 introduces unit tests so Node ABI rebuild is required before vitest; no E2E run expected at T8 (that lands at T10 verification).
Commit cadence: single chore(m35) atomic commit for the bump + badge-pin test; single chore(loki) ledger commit. Both same session. Atomic + ledger cadence held T1-T7; T8 preserves it.

Scope boundary: NO other version-string edits outside package.json + tests; NO CHANGELOG edits (T7 closed that surface); NO CLAUDE.md edits (T10 handles the Phase 5 COMPLETE line flip); NO retrospective edits. Bump + badge-pin test only. Target under 20 minutes.

Surprise watch: (a) root package.json may not carry a `version` field (pnpm workspace roots often omit it). If it does, bump. If it doesn't, document in the commit body. (b) the 'apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/components/app/top-bar.test.tsx' path is plan-speculative — confirm via `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'top-bar*'` before authoring; may live elsewhere. (c) landing-site `docs/site/index.html` is likely the biggest risk for a stale '1.0.0' reference — grep explicitly. (d) Plan-doc-vs-reality count drift: plan doc §3 T8 row says 'all 6 package.json files' but lists 7; honor the find-based repo-wide sweep over the static count.

Risk watch: If pnpm workspace root's package.json doesn't have a version field, the +1 unit test pinning `version === '1.1.0'` should target a file that definitively has it (apps/desktop/package.json is the safest anchor — it's the Electron app's own version and electron-builder uses it for installer naming). If reading via `fs.readFileSync` inside a vitest test, use `path.resolve(__dirname, ...)` to avoid CWD-dependent test fragility.
